rationale_for_baseline_tier: Benign pityriasis versicolor with body-image distress — routine reassurance and topical antifungal with a safety-net for atypical features; not urgent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Completely smooth scale-free depigmentation (possible vitiligo), or the rash blisters/becomes hot and painful or is associated with fever
escalation_edges.rationale: Scale-free depigmentation suggests vitiligo (different management); blistering/painful/febrile changes suggest another process.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pale patches are actually completely smooth (no scale), or the skin blisters, becomes hot and painful, or you get a fever, see your GP for reassessment.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: High body-image distress before a social event
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Acknowledge the distress and set clear, realistic expectations about the timeline for the marks to fade.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is pityriasis versicolor — a harmless yeast overgrowth. Use the medicated wash over your whole trunk/neck/arms as directed; the yeast dies within a week but the pale marks take months to fade. Shower after sweating and avoid occlusive clothing.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Routine GP in 4 weeks; sooner if the pattern suggests vitiligo or the skin becomes painful/blistered/febrile
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed unless blistering/painful skin or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document pityriasis versicolor (vitiligo/secondary syphilis screened), topical antifungal, expectation-setting, and recurrence-prevention advice.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Mislabelling scale-free depigmentation as PV (missing vitiligo) with no reassessment safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Vitiligo (with possible autoimmune associations) is mismanaged as PV and appropriate care/screening is delayed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No treatment/expectation-setting, leaving recurrence and distress.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot do a Wood's lamp/microscopy remotely to confirm PV vs vitiligo.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Elicit the branny scale to support PV, treat with a topical antifungal, set expectations, and keep a vitiligo-reassessment safety-net if the patches are truly scale-free.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Labelling as vitiligo without eliciting scale, or wrong expectations about repigmentation.
